title: Caveman Failed Signal Bar Trigger
focus_questions: Was the signal bar actually strong enough to trap real followers?
Did the trigger bar fully negate the signal bar, or only oppose it visually?

summary.thesis: A Caveman setup is an immediate failure of a strong signal bar, where trapped breakout followers fuel a fast opposite scalp.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trade every two-bar reversal. Only trade it when the signal bar was truly strong and the trigger bar objectively destroyed that thesis.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a strong breakout or continuation bar is immediately negated by a stronger opposite bar and the failed side has no quick rescue.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: failed breakout or late-trend continuation attempt near a meaningful edge. Required evidence: strong signal bar, trigger bar that closes beyond the signal bar's control point, and room for fast follow-through. Do not trade: in chop, with a weak signal bar, or when the trigger only partially negates the setup. Real trigger: full negation plus failed first rescue by the original side. Failure point: the original side immediately reclaims control or no fast follow-through appears.

when_relevant: A strong signal bar just attempted breakout or continuation from a meaningful area.
The opposite side immediately produced a stronger trigger bar that negates the original thesis.
The operator needs aggressive but still objective scalp-reversal logic.
required_evidence: the signal bar is strong, directional, and closes near its extreme
the trigger bar closes through the signal bar open or extreme strongly enough to prove failure
the setup occurs near an edge, failed breakout, or exhaustion area rather than inside random overlap
there is enough room for scalp or first target travel before major opposing structure
confirmation_signals: the trigger bar fully negates the signal bar's control point
the first retest fails to restore the original breakout direction
the opposite side gains immediate follow-through instead of collapsing into chop
invalidation_signals: the signal bar was weak and never trapped meaningful participation
the trigger bar does not truly negate the signal bar
the original direction quickly reclaims and holds after the trigger
no_trade_conditions: Do not use this setup if the signal bar itself lacks commitment.
Do not use it in overlapping chop or auction middle.
Do not trade it if the trigger only partially negates the signal bar.
Do not trade it if there is no room for a scalp target before major opposing structure.

entry_archetypes.name: trigger close reversal
entry_archetypes.description: Enter as the opposite trigger bar closes and objectively negates the prior signal bar.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the close is extended directly into the next major opposing barrier without room.

entry_archetypes.name: failed breakout retest
entry_archetypes.description: Enter on the first retest of the failed breakout point after the trigger bar already proved negation.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the retest restores the original breakout side instead of failing it.

management_notes: This is a fast-decay setup and usually needs quick follow-through.
Retest entries can be cleaner than emotional chase if the trigger bar already stretched.
Do not automatically convert scalp reversal into swing reversal without new evidence.
review_questions: Was the signal bar strong enough to trap real followers?
Did the trigger truly negate the signal bar or only pause it?
Was the trade taken at a meaningful edge or just inside noise?
Did the operator hold a scalp setup as if it were a guaranteed swing reversal?
